rulings.Case Title: MARIE LACEY VS. WEBCOR BUILERS INC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn To Compel Responses To Supplemental Interrogatories And Req.'S For Production
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, January 30, 2012, Line 1, DEFENDANTS WEBCOR CONSTRUCTION L.P., CUPERTINO ELECTRIC, INC, ANDREW M JORDAN, INC DBA A & B CONSTRUCTION, BRAND ENERGY SERVICES OF CALIFORNIA, INC's MOTION To Compel Responses To Supplemental Interrogatories And Req.'S For Production, Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: It appearing that there is no opposition to defendants' Webcor Construction, LP, Cupertino Electric, Inc, Andres M Jordan, Inc. DBA A&B Construction and Brand Energy Services of California, Inc. motion to compel responses to Supplemental Interrogatories and Requests for Production of Document and good cause appearing therefore: IT IS HEREBY ORDERED: (1) Plaintiff will provide complete, properly verified responses the Supplemental Interrogatories and Requests for Production of Documents without objections to defendants no later than February 7, 2012. (2) If Plaintiff fails to provide such responses to the Supplemental Interrogatories and Requests for Production of Documents within the time specified in Paragraph (1) above, Plaintiff will be precluded at trial for presenting any evidence beyond the evidence shown in response to the initial interrogatories and the initial request for production of documents." Defendant to prepare and submit order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rennepa@cooley.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/HK

rulings.Case Title: LOUIS GAMACHE et al VS. NICK VOLENTE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, January 30, 2012, Line 1. DEFENDANTS NICK VOLENTE, KAMRAN ARDEBILCHI AND JAHANGIR ARDEBILCHI'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Overruled as to the class allegations and the first, second, fifth and ninth causes of action, sustained with ten days leave to amend as to the third, fourth, tenth, eleventh, twelfth and thirteenth causes of action, and sustained without leave to amend as to the fourteenth cause of action. There is a reasonable probability that plaintiffs will be able to establish a community of interest and an ascertainable class. The first, second, fifth and ninth causes of action are adequately pled. Plaintiffs are granted leave to clarify how defendants interfered with their use and enjoyment of the premises and how these actions rendered the premises unfit for their intended purpose and constituted a nuisance; to plead their fourth claim with more specificity; to allege facts to support their tenth and eleventh claims; and to plead all of the requirements for an action that are set forth in Civil Code 1942.4(a). The fourteenth claim is duplicative of the first claim. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: LOUIS GAMACHE et al VS. NICK VOLENTE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, January 30, 2012, Line 1. DEFENDANT MORAN, MELVIN DBA BLUE LIGHT ELECTRIC, DBA MEGA DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Overruled, sustained with ten days leave to amend as to the second, eighth, tenth, eleventh and twelfth causes of action, sustained without leave to amend as to the fifth, seventh, thirteenth and fourteenth causes of action. There is a reasonable probability that Plaintiffs can establish a community of interest and an ascertainable class. Plaintiffs are granted leave to plead what duty was owed by this Defendant and how it was breached; to plead what extreme and outrageous conduct this Defendant committed; to plead facts to support the tenth and eleventh claims; to plead how the actions of this Defendant constitute a nuisance; . Constructive eviction results only from a landlord's material breach of the rental agreement. There is no relationship between this Defendant and Plaintiffs that supports the seventh claim. Civil Code 1942.4 only applies to landlords. The fourteenth claim is duplicative of the first claim. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: NYLIM REAL ESTATE VS. FRANK LEMBI et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, January 30, 2012, Line 5. PLAINTIFF NYLIM REAL ESTATE MEZZANINE FUND II, LP'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. The motion for summary judgment is granted and the motion for summary adjudication as to the defenses is off calendar as moot. Plaintiff is entitled to summary judgment on its two causes of action. Defendant received consideration for executing the Mezzanine Guaranties. Defendant fails to demonstrate that the default interest rate is a penalty. Defendant's causation argument lacks merit. Plaintiff to prepare a form of order and judgment that updates the interest amounts and insures that there is no double recovery Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)
